name: Action-Adventure
description: Focuses on combat and exploration with puzzle-solving elements. Examples include Tomb Raider and Uncharted.

name: First-Person Shooter
description: Player views the action through the eyes of the protagonist, using ranged weapons. Popularized by DOOM and Call of Duty.

name: Role-Playing Game
description: Character progression, quests, and storytelling. Includes subgenres like JRPG, ARPG, and CRPG.

name: Real-Time Strategy
description: Players control units and buildings in real-time to achieve objectives. StarCraft and Age of Empires are key examples.

name: Simulation
description: Replicates real-world activities or systems. Flight simulators, farming simulators, and city-building games belong here.

name: Survival Horror
description: Emphasizes resource management and fear, often with limited combat options. Resident Evil and Silent Hill define this genre.

name: Battle Royale
description: Large-scale PvP last-man-standing gameplay. Fortnite, PUBG, and Apex Legends are famous examples.

name: Roguelike
description: Features permadeath and procedural generation. Hades, The Binding of Isaac, and Dead Cells fit here.

name: Stealth
description: Avoiding detection by enemies, with gadgets and silent takedowns. Metal Gear Solid and Dishonored are key titles.

name: Open World
description: Large, freely explorable environments with nonlinear objectives. The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim and Grand Theft Auto V are iconic.

name: Metroidvania
description: Interconnected map with ability-gated progression. Hollow Knight and Castlevania: Symphony of the Night define this style.

name: Fighting
description: Close combat between characters with special moves. Street Fighter, Tekken, and Mortal Kombat lead this genre.

name: Puzzle
description: Focus on logic, pattern recognition, and problem-solving. Portal, Tetris, and The Witness are classic examples.

name: Racing
description: Competitive driving on tracks or open roads. Need for Speed, Forza Horizon, and Gran Turismo are popular.

name: Sandbox
description: Minimal goals, giving players freedom to create or destroy. Minecraft and Garry's Mod are prime examples.

name: Tactical RPG
description: Turn-based combat with strategic positioning. Fire Emblem and XCOM belong to this subgenre.

name: MMORPG
description: Massively multiplayer online role-playing with persistent worlds. World of Warcraft and Final Fantasy XIV lead this space.

name: Visual Novel
description: Story-driven with minimal gameplay, focusing on choices and dialogue. Doki Doki Literature Club and Steins,Gate are examples.

name: Rhythm
description: Players match inputs to music beats. Dance Dance Revolution, osu!, and Beat Saber fit here.

name: Party
description: Designed for local multiplayer with simple minigames. Mario Party and Jackbox Party Pack are iconic.

name: Horror
description: Focus on fear, dread, and psychological tension. Outlast and Amnesia: The Dark Descent are key titles.

name: Soulslike
description: High difficulty, stamina-based combat, and cryptic storytelling. Dark Souls, Bloodborne, and Elden Ring define this.

name: Walking Simulator
description: Exploration and narrative with minimal mechanics. Firewatch and What Remains of Edith Finch are examples.

name: Tower Defense
description: Build towers to stop enemies along a path. Kingdom Rush and Bloons TD are popular.

name: Card Battle
description: Collect and play cards to defeat opponents. Hearthstone, Magic: The Gathering Arena, and Gwent lead here.

name: Extraction Shooter
description: PvPvE with loot extraction mechanics. Escape from Tarkov and Hunt: Showdown are key examples.

name: Immersive Sim
description: Emergent gameplay with multiple solutions to problems. Deus Ex and Prey (2017) define this genre.

name: Life Simulation
description: Manage daily activities or relationships. The Sims and Animal Crossing are the most famous.

name: Sports
description: Simulates traditional sports gameplay. FIFA, NBA 2K, and Madden NFL belong here.

name: Hack and Slash
description: Fast-paced melee combat against many enemies. Devil May Cry and Dynasty Warriors are examples.
